name: Enterprise Deployment
headline: Create a higher-trust lane for governed rollout, deployment planning, and custom scope
idealFor: Best for internal platform teams and compliance-minded buyers
description: Enterprise Deployment creates the credible high-trust path for teams that need rollout planning, governance review, self-hosting options, or deeper operational support.
bullets: Support governed deployment and rollout review
Clarify security, hosting, and approval constraints early
Create larger engagement value with less commercial mismatch

qualificationChecks.title: Governance and approvals
qualificationChecks.description: Confirm how actions are approved, what control is required, and how operational risk is handled.

qualificationChecks.title: Deployment scope
qualificationChecks.description: Clarify whether the buyer needs self-hosting, deeper customization, or enterprise onboarding support.

qualificationChecks.title: Commercial fit
qualificationChecks.description: Move into scope only after rollout shape and operational requirements are understood.

timeline.title: Qualify the environment
timeline.description: Establish rollout, hosting, and governance requirements before scope is finalized.

timeline.title: Define the deployment path
timeline.description: Map the technical and operational work required to make the system fit the organization.

timeline.title: Move into scoped rollout
timeline.description: Turn the qualification process into a serious deployment plan once the fit is clear.

proofPoints.title: Qualification-first instead of forced checkout
proofPoints.description: Enterprise buyers need a route that respects rollout complexity and governance requirements.

proofPoints.title: A credible enterprise lane raises the value of everything else
proofPoints.description: The existence of a serious deployment path makes the entire product feel more durable and premium.

sections.title: Control and governance
sections.description: Enterprise stakeholders care about rollout clarity, approvals, and where the system runs.

sections.title: Customization and onboarding
sections.description: More complex teams need deeper support connecting the system to internal operations and release workflows.

sections.title: Longer-term value
sections.description: A serious enterprise lane improves commercial credibility even for buyers who start smaller.

leadForm.heading: Submit the enterprise qualification brief
leadForm.intro: Use this form to outline governance, hosting, rollout, and customization requirements before a commercial path is proposed.

cta.heading: Use enterprise deployment when the rollout needs more than a normal checkout path.
cta.body: This lane is intentionally built around qualification first so the final scope is grounded in operational reality.
